varieties.name: Typica
varieties.origin: Ethiopia/Yemen, 1600s; Australia since 1880s-1920s, reestablished 1980s
varieties.traits: Tall plants (3.5-5m), conical shape, bronze leaf tips, elongated cherries, wide spacing required
varieties.yield_notes: Low productivity (20-30% less than Bourbon), susceptible to pests/diseases

varieties.name: K7 (Kenya Selection 7)
varieties.origin: Kenya, 1930s; introduced to Australia 1990s
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, rust-resistant, drought-tolerant, well-suited to Australian dry conditions
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, suitable for mechanical harvesting

varieties.name: SL6 (Scott Laboratories 6)
varieties.origin: Kenya Scott Agricultural Laboratories, 1930s; Australia 1990s-2000s
varieties.traits: Drought-resistant, adaptable to dry conditions, moderate vigor
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high productivity, reliable cropper

varieties.name: SL14 (Scott Laboratories 14)
varieties.origin: Kenya Scott Laboratories, 1930s; Australia 2000s
varieties.traits: Bronze leaf tips, good branching structure, moderate size
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity for quality-focused cultivation
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically recommended for cooler subtropical areas of Northern NSW and Southern Queensland including humid subtropical climates region. Preferred for warmer sites within subtropical zones. Part of cultivar recommendations from Australian agricultural research. Kenyan heritage provides quality genetics adapted to subtropical conditions. Suited to Humid subtropical cooler winter temperatures and moderate climate. Performs well in Northern Rivers Area and SE Queensland plantations.
varieties.availability: Queensland and NSW coffee nurseries, research station supplies

varieties.name: Red Catuai (Catuai Rojo)
varieties.origin: Brazil IAC cross (Mundo Novo x Caturra), 1949-1972; Australia 1980s-1990s
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf stature (2-3m), dense branching, red cherries, wind-resistant
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, responds well to fertilization, suitable for dense planting

varieties.name: Yellow Catuai (Catuai Amarillo)
varieties.origin: Brazil, yellow-fruited mutation of Catuai; Australia 1990s-2000s
varieties.traits: Compact growth, yellow cherries when ripe, dense branching
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, similar to Red Catuai, dense planting compatible
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for northern Queensland including humid subtropical climates region. Yellow fruit provides visual distinction in gardens. Produces quality beans for roasting with good cup profile. Highly ornamental in subtropical gardens with bright yellow cherries. Can withstand Humid subtropical heat and humidity. Suitable for both commercial and backyard cultivation. Compact size ideal for small properties. Shares Red Catuai's high yield and climate adaptability. Can experience unpleasant oily taste when cold, best consumed fresh in warm climate.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (NSW/QLD), specialty fruit tree suppliers

varieties.name: Mundo Novo
varieties.origin: Brazil natural hybrid (Typica x Bourbon), discovered 1943, distributed 1952
varieties.traits: Tall vigorous plants, large trees, moderate ripening, bronze to green young leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity (20-30% more than Typica), disease-resistant

varieties.name: CRB (Local Selection)
varieties.origin: Australia local selection developed for cooler subtropical sites
varieties.traits: Adapted to cooler locations, moderate growth, suitable for southern Queensland
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable cropping in cooler subtropical areas

varieties.name: Kieperson
varieties.origin: Unknown/uncertain, recommended for Australian conditions
varieties.traits: Adapted to Queensland climate, moderate vigor
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in Queensland conditions

varieties.name: Blue Mountain (Typica Variant)
varieties.origin: Jamaica Blue Mountains, Typica lineage; Australia 1990s-2000s
varieties.traits: Tall plants, bronze new growth, less dense habit than K7, open branching
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate (less than K7), matures earlier than K7

varieties.name: Bourbon
varieties.origin: Yemen to Bourbon Island (Réunion) 1708, French missionaries; Australia via Papua New Guinea
varieties.traits: Tall plants, wider leaves than Typica, larger cherries, conical shape, 20-30% higher yield than Typica
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, 20-30% more than Typica

varieties.name: Caturra
varieties.origin: Brazil, natural Bourbon mutation discovered 1930s; Australia 1990s-2000s
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf growth (2-3m), wide waxy wavy leaves, bushy appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Higher than Bourbon due to compact size allowing dense planting

varieties.name: Arusha
varieties.origin: Tanzania, Bourbon/Typica lineage; Australia via Papua New Guinea
varieties.traits: Small beans, unique growth characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity

varieties.name: K7 Variegated (Decorative Sport)
varieties.origin: Australia, variegated sport mutation of K7
varieties.traits: Smaller variety, stable variegation pattern, prefers dappled shade, ornamental foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than standard K7, focus on ornamental value

varieties.name: CRB (Coffee Research Brisbane)
varieties.origin: Australia, developed by NSW Agriculture Department, extensive trials Alstonville NSW
varieties.traits: Open spreading variety, resistant to Berry Disease and Coffee Rust, reliable cropper
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable consistent cropping, recommended for mechanical harvesting

varieties.name: Kent
varieties.origin: India (Mysore Doddengudda Estate) 1911, discovered by British planter L.P. Kent
varieties.traits: Typica mutation, rust-resistant selection, tall vigorous plants, adapted to monsoon climate
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, historically popular through 1920s-1930s, exceptional cup quality

varieties.name: S.795 (Selection 795)
varieties.origin: India Balehonnur Coffee Research Station, 1940s (Kent x S.288 hybrid)
varieties.traits: Tall vigorous growth, high yields, bold beans, rust-resistant, superior quality
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, reliable cropper, responds well to fertilization

varieties.name: Cauvery (Catimor)
varieties.origin: India, cross between Caturra and Hybrido-de-Timor (CCRI Selection 12)
varieties.traits: Natural Bourbon mutant (Caturra) crossed with Timor Hybrid, high yield, rust-resistant, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, consistent cropping, responds to intensive management

varieties.name: Selection 9 (Sln.9)
varieties.origin: India CCRI cross (Tafarikela Ethiopian Arabica x Hybrido-de-Timor)
varieties.traits: Tall vigorous plants, drooping branches, medium to long internodes, dark bronze young leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High (1700 kg/ha average over 23 years), early ripening, drought hardy

varieties.name: Monsoon Malabar (Monsooned Processing)
varieties.origin: India Malabar Coast (tropical monsoon climates, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu Nilgiris), historical British Raj era shipping process

varieties.name: San Ramon (Dwarf/Semi-Dwarf)
varieties.origin: Costa Rica, dwarf mutation; introduced to India for high-density plantings
varieties.traits: Dwarf to semi-dwarf growth, compact size for dense planting, manageable height
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in high-density systems

varieties.name: Selection 5A (Sln.5A)
varieties.origin: India cross (Devamachy x S.881 Wild Arabica Rume Sudan)
varieties.traits: Vigorous vegetative growth, erect branching, small narrow elongated leathery leaves with wavy margin, green leaf tips
varieties.yield_notes: Good potential 1200-1500 kg/ha, field rust resistance

varieties.name: Selection 5B (Sln.5B)
varieties.origin: India cross (Devamachy x S.333)
varieties.traits: Related to Sln.5A, derived from different Devamachy cross
varieties.yield_notes: Similar productivity to Sln.5A family

varieties.name: Chandragiri (Sln.13, Sarchimor)
varieties.origin: India CCRI from Portugal CRRC (Villasarchi x Hybrido-de-Timor), released 2007
varieties.traits: Sarchimor type, fairly rust-resistant, high-yielding, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, consistent cropping

varieties.name: Robusta Parchment
varieties.origin: India, Coffea canephora (Robusta) species
varieties.traits: Robust growth, disease-resistant, drought-tolerant, thrives in lowland conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, reliable cropper

varieties.name: Kaapi Royale (Robusta Specialty)
varieties.origin: India premium Robusta selection
varieties.traits: Superior Robusta quality, selected for premium characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: High Robusta productivity

varieties.name: Peridenia (Robusta)
varieties.origin: India Robusta variety for commercial production
varieties.traits: Robust commercial Robusta characteristics, disease-resistant
varieties.yield_notes: High commercial productivity

varieties.name: S.724 (Robusta Selection)
varieties.origin: India Coffee Board selection program
varieties.traits: Selected Robusta with improved characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable high productivity

varieties.name: C x R (Congensis x Robusta)
varieties.origin: India hybrid cross Coffea congensis x Coffea canephora robusta
varieties.traits: Interspecific hybrid, combines characteristics of both parents
varieties.yield_notes: Good hybrid vigor, reliable production

varieties.name: Mysore Nuggets Extra Bold
varieties.origin: India (Mysore region Karnataka, adjacent to tropical monsoon climates)
varieties.traits: Premium grade designation, extra bold bean size, high quality
varieties.growing_notes: Specialty brand representing India's premium coffee from Mysore (Karnataka) and tropical monsoon climates Nilgiris/Shevaroys regions. 'Nuggets' refers to premium extra bold bean grading. Can exhibit higher acidity than typical south Indian coffees. Famous Indian specialty coffee with international recognition. Grown in southern districts of Nilgiris and Shevaroys adjacent to tropical monsoon climates. Represents premium quality segment of Indian coffee. Important specialty product for tropical monsoon climates and neighboring regions. Available for tropical monsoon climates specialty market. Commands premium prices in international markets.
varieties.availability: Premium tropical monsoon climates and Karnataka estates, specialty Indian coffee exporters

varieties.name: Bababudan Giris (Regional Origin)
varieties.origin: India Baba Budan Giri hills (Karnataka/tropical monsoon climates border), where coffee was first planted in India 1670
varieties.traits: Heritage varieties from original Indian coffee region, historical significance

varieties.name: Coorg Coffee (Regional Designation)
varieties.origin: India Kodagu (Coorg) district Karnataka, adjacent to tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Regional varieties from India's largest coffee-producing district, multiple variety types

varieties.name: Wayanad Coffee (Regional Kerala Designation)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Wayanad district (now part of tropical monsoon climates, historically Malabar), spread from Karnataka 1840

varieties.name: Travancore Coffee (Historical Kerala Designation)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates erstwhile princely state of Travancore (southern tropical monsoon climates including tropical monsoon climates region)
varieties.traits: Regional designation covering Idukki (mostly Robusta) and Nelliampathys (Arabica and Robusta)

varieties.name: Malabar Arabica/Robusta (Coastal Designation)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Malabar Coast (historical name for tropical monsoon climates coastal region)

varieties.name: Liberica (Coffea liberica)
varieties.origin: West Africa Liberia, introduced to India in response to coffee rust
varieties.traits: Large leaves and cherries, tall trees, highly disease-resistant, low altitude tolerance

varieties.name: Excelsa (Coffea liberica var. dewevrei)
varieties.origin: West/Central Africa, now considered Liberica variety; limited India cultivation
varieties.traits: Similar to Liberica, large leaves, disease-resistant, lowland tolerance
